name: The Learning Enrichment Foundation
address: 116 Industry Street, Suite 2028, Toronto, Ontario M6M 4L8
description:
The Best Computer lesson for Seniors. God bless and More power LEF...

Honestly I'd never ever call this agency for any program . I have tried to get into the ECA program for more than 2 years I think even before the pandemic, and after countless times of trying to get a hold if the program coordinator, I was being transferred all over the place, they told me they would put my name on a waiting list, so when the program became available I'd be called( that never happend).kept leaving messages for MARLENE who never ever reaches out to you back. This organization is incompetent, and misleading. So I tried again after covid same thing until surprisingly, she emailed back because of the referral being sent. Don't waste your time trying to get into programs here. When you do call for info they don't want to tell you anything more than call Marlene who never responds. Regardless of who is eligible or not ypu all should have a decent policy that allows you to get some type of insight about your services. How can a business flourish when you guys treat potential clients awful. They are even worst than Walmart associates. I'd never ever call this place again. I sent her an email and it's been months since ,and I know I'd never get a response. So koodos to your company maybe it just wasn't meant to be.  And btw stop telling people that a program will start at a certain time,and know it will never. Glad to see I'm not the only one who gets no correspondence from these people. And clearly they pock and choose who to contact back,and who to help.

This daycare is the best! All staff is amazing! My two favourite staff is Evelyn and Simone! Their energy level is amazing and how they treated my daughter with so much care and love. I use to call every day to check on my daughter and they didnt mind, always informed me on everything to do with my daughter. My daughter left in 2019 and she even cry when she was leaving. I met to put this review a long time ago but i just remembered. Highly recommed this daycare, the staff will treat your children with love and care. Amazing people. My daughter goes to another daycare because we moved but i wish i could take her back to this daycare.

I am really not sure how this place has 5 star reviews and wish there would be more honest reviews for people like myself who chose this place because of the good reviews. My child would come home with snot on his face, dirt all over his clothes. They said that they would help to support him during potty training but they would send him home in diapers and not even send home his soiled clothes for a couple days until I had to call the director and let her know what was going on. She was helpful however they continued to send him home with his soiled clothes from the day before and not from the same day so his clothes would be sitting in a bag in urine for 2 days at a time. They would also send him home with clothes that were not even his.They would only update the daycare app once a week which is really not helpful considering during covid we can’t go inside to pick up our children and get daily updates. They also said due to covid they need to have the kids outdoor learning majority of the day. They bring them out in the morning and then again at 3 until you pick them up so in the winter cold months they were outside for so long. Nothing they were doing was sitting well with me so after a month I transferred him to another centre that didn’t have any reviews which was why I chose this one to begin with. Best decision trusting my gut and taking my child out of there. The new place is amazing they are so clean, don’t keep the kids outside for hours, support the potty training process, treat kids like their own and give you daily updates and pics. Hoping this honest review will help someone else make an informative choice since our children are the most important thing in the world and we want the absolute best for them.

I cannot say enough positive things about Donald C. MacDonald and their outstanding staff! As a small business owner, Covid forced me back to work long before I was ready - my son started daycare at just 3 months old. I was extremely nervous and hesitant, as we were not allowed to enter the center due to their newly set health measures. Denise went above and beyond to provide a thorough presentation and answer all my questions so I felt at ease. Now 6 months later, my son has THRIVED at Donald C! He is so happy and smiling ear to ear each and every day. He is learning so much and hitting all of his required milestones, which the staff continue to keep me updated on. Every time I receive a notification from Storypark, it puts a huge smile on my face!

In a year where we have not been able to see many friends or family, I am grateful for his exposure to other children and the incredible educators he is surrounded by daily! They have always kept me informed daily, and try extremely hard to stick to our set sleep schedule (a huge feat when caring for 9 other infants). They have also kept us confident and informed as Covid waves have peaked, and supported our family in making decisions within these difficult times. I feel extremely confident in their cleanliness and care of our babies! They care for my son as if he is one of their own. Lastly, you cannot help but smile every morning when greeted by Simone at the door - her energy is palpable, and she has made so many hard days (when I don't want to say goodbye to my sweet boy), so much easier!

name: The Neighbourhood Group Community Services (TNGCS)
address: 1415 Bathurst Street Toronto, Ontario M5R 3H8
description:
I worked with Erick Rocha and Ronaldo Briceno; both were so respectful and helpful.
Erick helped me find a job in less than a week.
He helped me to create a cover letter and a professional resume
The whole crew was so professional, caring and helpful.

Very disappointing experience. They want you to register and attend information sessions and workshops, and then refuse to help you, providing services only to privileged groups. They only care to report the desired number of people and get the money. I wouldn't recommend this place to anyone.

I wanted to share some feedback about my recent meeting with Rosa Henriquez. Unfortunately, it wasn’t a positive experience.

During our meeting, Rosa was quite rude and dismissive, which made the whole interaction uncomfortable. Her tone and approach felt unprofessional, and there was a noticeable lack of empathy. This was especially disappointing since it was my first Metting with her , and I had hoped for a more welcoming and respectful atmosphere.

I wanted to bring this to your attention because I believe interactions like this can leave a negative impression. I trust this isn’t reflective of how the organization typically operates.

Awesome staff!!! Very professional but more importantly, respectful and kind. I felt sincerity in each interaction. I went there to help with job searching. The staff member I spoke with was very helpful. I feel confident in finding placement in the work force through this organization. Highly recommended if your on the hunt for gainful employment.

The Good: The facility space is welcoming and warm.
The Great: The staff commitment and teamwork is impeccable.
The Critique: Due to limited funding and high demands for jobs, this place can be both a place of happiness and severe disappointment.
My Recommendation: Come here with an open mind, and be wiling to do your part in helping the Staff, help you.

name: Thornhill Employment Hub
address: 8199 Yonge Street, Unit 101B, Thornhill, Ontario L3T 2C6
description:
I had a wonderful experience working with Lisa Ceresney at The Thornhill Employment Hub. She was incredibly supportive and professional throughout my job search process.

Lisa provided me with excellent resources, helped me improve and refine my resume, and guided me step by step until I successfully secured a job. Her knowledge, patience, and genuine willingness to help made a big difference for me.

I truly appreciate her support and highly recommend her services to anyone looking for employment assistance.

I had an appointment with Muina today at 11:00 AM. There was heavy snow, so I checked my email repeatedly all morning to see if any appointments were cancelled, but there was no notice at all.

So, I left home at 9:30 AM, dealt with a major GO Transit delay, and had to pay for two Ubers to make it on time.
When I arrived, surprisingly the center was closed. I waited 20 minutes, called, emailed, rang the doorbell but nothing. So frustrated so disappointed.

Then at 11:45 AM, they called me to say, Sorry, we’re closed today!

That’s not a minor inconvenience that an apology fixes. It’s a clear lack of basic responsibility and respect, especially toward unemployed clients who can hardly afford last-minute transportation costs.

If you’re going to close, notify people in advance. This was careless and unacceptable👎

My consultant, Lisa Ceresney, helped me get a job within just a few weeks of starting after months of unsuccessful searching. She helped me with my resume hands-on and got my dream job quickly. Thanks team!

The Thornhill Employment Hub is a great resource. Valentyna was such a great support for my father. She helped with so much patience and kindness and we truly felt cared of and respected throughout the process. We're so thankful for everything that she has done. The Thornhill Employment Hub is lucky to have her.

I am an IT Professional
Recently unemployed  and have not updated my resume in 10 years  ( I was working)
I knew Market Trends in my field are constantly changing and required my Resume to reflect that,  so I needed a professional
with the latest Resume formats and expertise  in the marketplace.
10 years ago Thornhill Hub did that with great success so I went back and met with Natasha.
Wow!!  so helpful and understanding I felt I had a team on this journey She was fantastic...
...my approach  No ego  let the professionals guide you they know the market  and do the homework Natasha assigns you :)
Steven Aug 2025 ... i start Sept 2 2025

name: Times Change Women’s Employment Service
address: 2 Carlton Street, Suite 701, Toronto, Ontario M5B 1J3
description:
I am really glad I visited Times Change Women’s Employment Service today for the first time. I felt very positive and welcomed. The team is doing wonderful work by supporting women with job search guidance and other important areas of life. I am looking forward to starting my job-hunt journey with them soon and truly believe their support will help me find a good opportunity soon. Thank you, Times Change whole team! 😇

Having previously experienced another employment service, I can truly say that Times Change feels very different. Even though I’ve only recently started with this service, I already see how caring and dedicated the team is in helping clients reach their goals. I’m especially grateful for Neda’s support, she has helped me greatly along this journey and given me a renewed sense of hope.

I'm so thankful to Times Change for helping me find a job. My counsellor, Neda, believed in me more than I believed in myself. Her support and encouragement gave me the confidence I needed to move forward.  Even months later, I still reach out to her for guidance. I truly appreciate everything she and the team did for me

I can't recommend Isabella highly enough. Her support has not only helped me solve technical issues but has genuinely opened my mind to new possibilities. She's incredibly patient, knowledgeable, and clear in her explanations—making even the most complex topics easy to understand.

Each session left me feeling more confident and empowered with technology. What sets Isabella apart is her ability to tailor the learning experience to your pace and needs. She's not just fixing problems; she's helping you grow.

If you're looking for reliable, insightful, and truly transformative tech support or training, Isabella is the one to call. Thank you, Isabella, for your dedication and for helping me see technology in a whole new light!

Attending the CAMP career program with Times Change has been life transforming for me. I was blown away by the step by step, inspiring practical content, and the supportive, Values and Vision driven coaching provided by Shari. The amazing community of women was a key element on our journey transitioning from fears and doubts to clarity and confidence. The best gift at the end of our program …our group continues to get together,  support and empower each other. I also see incredible benefits from attending high quality workshops, and working with educational and employment counsellors, one on one. I would highly recommend Times Change to any woman of any age who wants to get clarity and confidence pursuing a meaningful career aligned with their strengths, gifts, values.

name: TNO- The Neighbourhood Organization
address: 45 Overlea Blvd, Unit 200, East York Town Centre, Toronto, ON M4H 1C3
description:
I volunteered here with some work colleagues and had a real appreciation of the volunteers at this food bank. It's very well organized and has a reasonable stock of food items. What I really appreciated was the respect and dignity exhibited towards recipients. It is indeed commendable how this operation is being managed so diligently.  Thanks to all the volunteers that are working at TNO and supporting a noble cause.

In food bank they need front desk some capable person who handle everything without any mistakes.in yesterday I go food bank collect my baby diapers they said I collect them already but I am not collecting yesterday I go first time in this month . When I talked with coordinator she told me they r mistake. It’s not good thing. But front desk people showing attitude they r not mistake anything . Really very disappointing how much incapable person handle front desk

Horrendous Service Experience
I'm writing to express extreme dissatisfaction following what transpired on Saturday July 26th, I sent an email the following day to raise a complaint about the person who I spoke with over the phone almost 2 weeks prior to my visit on July 26th but no one followed up with me as they promised they'll do.

The week of July 14th I completed registration over the phone with an employee named Adeela (that's what the name sounded to me over the phone). She said she had to complete an intake form for me before I could proceed to speak to someone. I clearly explained to her what my needs are in terms of mom-baby support (besides the Early on program) prior to completing registration and she confirmed that I would get that type of help at TNO. When I finished answering all her questions, she mentioned to me that the next step is to book me with a counselor, I told her I can only come on Saturday so she advised July 26th would be good. A counselor will be present to attend to me and provide me with the support I need.

On July 26th I expected a very seamless experience heading to the TNO but I wasn't prepared for the horrendous service experience awaiting me.
I spent the whole morning and part of my afternoon until 2pm trying to find out where I had to go and who would attend to my questions. I ran between the two locations: I went to 10 Gate Blvd and found the office closed and came to 45 Overlea Blvd. I found staff there but I wasn't registered and my name appeared nowhere. Staff on site managed to get me in to speak to a settlement counselor on duty but they said there is nothing they can support with as they are new at TNO and they're specialized in settlement not family services.

All this was happening while I was dragging my 6 month old baby with me as I had no one to watch him and care for him in my absence. Not only my time and efforts were wasted but also baby's time: time to get fed, time to get changed, time to get a nap.

I'm appalled by the indifference, apathy, frustrating, shameful, and disgusting service I witnessed at the hands of your organization which is supposed to do just the opposite: Provide support, genuine understanding, empathy, warmth , generosity and a sense of community.

I requested that someone review my concerns and get back to me soon with a resolution. My concerns are still unresolved to this day, almost  a month after I reported this incident. Why? Because simply no one cares, I'm just a number to help boost their intake data.

Disappointing and Unprofessional Experience with Caseworker

Today I visited TNO Employment Services hoping to apply for Better Jobs Ontario funding. I was assigned to a caseworker named Abdul Hafiz. Unfortunately, my experience was disappointing and felt dismissive from the beginning.

After greeting me, Mr. Hafiz led me to a room and gave me the silent treatment for several minutes. He did not explain the purpose of the meeting or ask any meaningful questions to understand my goals. When he finally spoke, he asked abruptly why I was there — even though I had already explained I was there to apply for Better Jobs Ontario to support my full-time PSW program.

He filled out only the first page of my application, left much of it incomplete, and then told me he would refer me to a settlement worker — someone who is not responsible for the actual funding process. I left confused, unsupported, and feeling like my time was wasted.

As a single parent with two children and a limited window to apply for funding while in school, I expected professionalism, clarity, and support — none of which were provided in this meeting.

I’m now seeking help elsewhere and hope that others won’t face the same experience I did. I
strongly recommend better training and oversight to ensure clients are treated with respect and that critical services like Better Jobs Ontario are handled properly.

— Zeinab Ahmed

The Best Organization !
I had a very good experience there—it’s truly wonderful organization. Nora, Nosheen Khan, Alexandra Bedoev, Maiko Taku, Vijaya Gowri Mohan, Husam(Sam), Mahmoud  Zeineh are the best people. They helped me and gave me a new life. Thanks to all of them! So helpful 🙏🏻🧿✨

name: Toronto Community Employment Services
address: 1 Eglinton Ave East Suite 703 Toronto, ON M4P 3A1
description:
I wanted to take a moment to sincerely thank Tania at TCES for everything you’ve done to support me during what was a very challenging and uncertain period in my life.

For over a year that I was searching for a permanent and reliable role, you were there every step of the way. You truly took the time to understand my needs, from commute and workplace environment to compatibility with my education, experience, and long-term goals. Your thoughtful and holistic approach made me feel seen, supported, and genuinely cared for.

Navigating severance while searching for new opportunities was not easy, but your patience, encouragement, and steady guidance made a real difference. You always listened carefully, offered honest and insightful advice, and helped point me in the right direction when I needed it most. Your support helped me stay focused and positive, even during moments of doubt.

Now I have secured a new position, and I truly know that your help played a big part in getting me here. I wouldn’t be where I am today without your dedication and commitment, and I am deeply grateful for everything you’ve done.

Thank you Tania, again for your unwavering support, professionalism, and kindness. It has meant more to me than I can properly put into words.

Warmest regards,
Mary Sayyad

I wanted to sincerely thank you to Tania for all the support you gave me during a very difficult period in my life. Your patience, encouragement, and guidance made a real difference while I was going through severance and searching for new opportunities.You always took the time to listen, provide thoughtful advice, and point me in the right direction. Your support helped me stay focused and positive, and I’m truly grateful for that.I’m happy to share that I’ve now gotten a job, and I know your help played a big part in getting me here. Thank you for everything.
Regard,
Azar M.

I had a great experience with them. I’m lucky to have Tania as my mentor, she is wonderful and considerate. She gave me hope again and helped me find a job the same day I met her. She keeps following up with me and checking if everything is going well. I highly recommend consulting with her because she really cares about helping you. Thank you Tania l’m so grateful for your support 🙏🏻

While sitting on my Couch and thinking what is my next step because I felt like the whole world was tumbling down on my shoulder.  I decided to contact TCES. And I spoke with one of their counselors Tania a wonderful and beautiful lady with excellent customer service . , she motivated me and inspired me . Tania  made me feel so safe and made me  realize that they would help me get that job. When I left her office my hope came alive again . Tania listens and understands situations , and give good advice . that I cannot give up but to pushed myself towards my goals .
I Thank TCES for putting my resume together and booking appointments with me in person and within two weeks I got my career job . I thank God and I could not do it without TCES. Thanks  much  to Tania and TCES for great reception their  service is excellent .
Please dont give up go and search for help and make your first step be with tces they are great!!!
Merle Gordon

Thanks TCES for every ❤️

As a newcomer in Canada, TCES was my support system. From the very beginning, the staff were kind and helpful. I was lucky to have been mentored by Tania and Adma, who have always guided and motivated me throughout the past 10 months. I also want to mention and thank Kamuran, who helped me through mock interviews. They have helped me from resume drafting to mock interviews, guidance, job search, follow up, etc. I felt they are genuine and straightforward in their approach. I am grateful for their services and have huge respect for their hard work and efforts in helping me and those like me. I would strongly recommend Toronto Community Employment Services to anyone who is searching for a start in Canada.

name: Toronto District School Board – Next Steps Employment
address: 3850 Sheppard Ave East, Scarborough, Ontario M1T 3L4
description:
As an Afghan citizen who has been living in Canada for two years, I have always wondered how to continue my career in the field I love and which is my profession. Fortunately, I came to the Next Steps Employment Center and was introduced to the very kind Ms. Mary Beltran. With her hard work and support, my Better Job Ontario application was approved. So I would like to thank Mary Beltran, she is wonderful. I appreciated you, dear Mary.

After being unemployed for many months I've decided to enter into this Place "Better Jobs Ontario. After gathering much info. on my first meeting and attending sessions. I must say I felt well supported by Mary Beltran, I am please with the service I received. Mary was always pleasant and effective in answering questions and returning calls in a timely manner. The process takes time, you just have to be patient. I am overall satified and pleased with the service.
Thankyou for all that you have done for me. Greatly appreciate it.
Forever Greatful MS.

I recently attended the resume and cover letter workshop and found it extremely helpful and informative. Juie Bakth the Employment Specialist was exceptional, knowledgeable, engaging, and very approachable. She explained key concepts clearly, shared valuable tips, and provided practical examples that made the learning experience very effective. I feel much more confident about improving my resume. Thank you Juie for an excellent session!

I am very grateful to my agent Mary, who helped me organize all the application materials and drafted various documents for me. My application took more than 7 months. When I was about to give up, she encouraged me and communicated with the BJO office in a timely manner. She even helped me check the status every week. Thank her very much! My application success is entirely due to Mary. She is very professional, experienced, and has a great attitude. I am lucky to have such an agent. Thank you very much, Mary!

it was a pleasure dealing with Mary Beltran at the Scarborough office.  I was provided with all the information and the steps required to get in to the BJO program , and i am now half way through my education in Occupational Health and Safety .  Every one at the office  was very helpful and supportive in helping me on my journey .  I shall reach out Mary once my course is finished so she may assist me in finding a job in the field of my studies .
Thanks a bunch Mary .

name: Toronto District School Board – Next Steps Employment
address: 3030 Don Mills Road, North York, Ontario M2J 3K3
description:
I am grateful that I joined the Next Step Employment Centre. They help me develop my resume, help me with work clothes and overall job hunting strategies. I was able to land on a contract job while I was enrolled at Next Step Employment Centre. I would recommend it to anyone who are serious about finding jobs. I had a successful interview and a job offer because I listened to thier advice.

I found this employment as I was applying for the open positions on the TDSB website. I am glad to have found this employment centre and have met my mentor, Earl. Through our 1-on-1 meeting and connecting via email, Earl had helped me find a job position to apply to, and was able to edit my resume to match the job description. Before I accessed the employment centre, I was getting ghosted and received automatic email rejections from the jobs I applied to for months. It caused me stress, and I felt hopeless…

I am now employed, and am looking forward to the continuous support (12 months period) this employment centre offers to job seekers. I recommend this employment centre to job seekers of all fields, whether you are a fresh graduate, a newcomer to Canada, or someone who has been unemployed and returning to work after years of not working. Take advantage of the service and it is free of charge too!

My late father's wisdom words he used to say to me back in Sierra Leone, "Empty the Cup" and "To Take in the New," is truly inspiring. It's a great reminder to stay open-minded, receptive, and adaptable, especially in new and unfamiliar situations. When I first walked into Next-Steps I met an amazing friendly lady Cenn, I had a warm welcome which instantly break my fears as a newcomer and feel at home, so I stayed open to take in the next new information. Next I met with Isabel Fonseca, who became my Employment Coach, she showed me multiples of choices as a newcomer in Canada with honesty. She has and still provided me with professional and personal kindness at every given time,which makes love Canada the more. This led me to another amazing woman Ann M. She is fun with very good character, she made me understand the importance of knowing how to write my resume and cover letter, she thought me about cultural working differences, how to be bold with eye contact which was a big deal for me based on my culture, and also how to take pride in my motivation and my work ethic. Then back to my Employment Coach Isabel for additional support and guidance, Next Step should be proud for having her given her best . My contact with Next Steps is a continuing journey in this new country. As a newcomer in Canada my best and honest advice is try Next steps for career pathway.

Next Steps Employment helped me to find a job when I was struggling to fond work. I met with Isabel who worked with me to understand the job market and how to show my skills on my resume and interview skills. The centre provided me the opportunity to meet with employers at a job fair where I was able to connect with hiring companies and was offered employment. They followed up with me and gave me assistance and support in my journey to finding work. The people were all very friendly and helpful and I appreciate their efforts. I would recommend them for those looking for help in finding employment.

My name is Rose. Next Steps Downsview Center has been nothing short of a godsend! I was greeted in a friendly manner and received excellent service throughout my time there. I received help with my resume and other tips (like help with job interviews). I would not be where I am today without Next Steps. They helped me get second career so I could go back to school. If it wasn't for Next Steps I would not have the job I have now making more money with a really great boss. I can honestly say that the center and all the stuff made a huge difference in my life.

name: Toronto District School Board – Next Steps Employment
address: 1000 Gerrard Street East, 2nd floor, Toronto, Ontario M4M 3G6
description:
This Centre has offered me outstanding support over the past year and I would highly recommend them to anyone experiencing a career transition.  The team (Roger, Alex, Erica, Andrea and others) has been proactive, professional, and positive in offering a wide range of support such as career coaching, resume best practices, interview tips, George Brown Career Choices workshop, financial aid, and ongoing check-ins. This Centre is not a recruitment agency that actively finds you work (that is your responsibility as you would know best how to do this). Rather this Centre provides coaching, counselling, and content to enable your job and career search more effectively. The Ontario government needs to invest more into Centres and services like this especially during this disruptive time of Tarriffs, AI, and Ageism which is causing more job upheaval. Thank you Team Next Steps!

Had a great experience with this agency. Joined earlier this year and was able to obtain something! Received help with my resume, job leads, attended workshops, got help when I asked, received great advice - and it all culminated successfully. Linda was consistent with sending job leads and providing great advice, Alex lead great workshops and followed up which was nice, Rod provided detailed information and assistance, and Andrea was very considerate. A great team to work with if you are willing to also put in the work!

Linda Martel! Thank you for your hard work and support in helping me transition smoothly from a Driver to a Project Coordinator role. Your guidance throughout my job search journey made a real difference.

I am very grateful to her and the Next-Steps Employment Centre for their hard work and support throughout my job search journey. In January 2025, Linda recommended the Humber College Bridging Program and connected me to the program coordinator, which helped me organize and focus on my goals. Well done!

I'd like to thank all the team at next steps. Employment center Riverdale. Over the past 3 months I've been working with the team here as I was looking for employment. Working with the team, I had the opportunity to work on developing my resume and my interview skills. And just within the last week I was able to connect with a local employer. They looked at my resume. They interviewed me and I got a job on the spot. I'd like to acknowledge Roger and Rod for all their help

In 6.5 months coming to Canada, for the first time in my life with an Open Work Permit, I got my first job all thanks to Andrea Tagalakis in NSEC Riverdale. Before NSEC Riverdale, I reached out to many other recruiters but they only work with Citizen, PR, or Asylum Seekers. Riverdale enrolled me and Andrea looked into my whole career profile and told me what to and not to do without any sugarcoating. In times when Ontario has the highest unemployment rate, finding a job and then getting more training in different areas was a truly a blessing. Now I am working at GardaWorld pursuing my aspiration for a future in law enforcement.

name: Toronto District School Board – Next Steps Employment
address: 6435 Erin Mills Parkway, Units C-03 and C-02, Mississauga ON LHN 4H4 (Battleford Centre)
description:
Judith is one of the best this agency has! She is super knowledgable and her dedication to help you is simple amazing. She works from her heart and it shows. She helped me show the ropes of how resume writing works and i loved attending her workshops. She spent one on one time with me coaching me even though i wasn’t assigned to her. I had Genevieve who seriously wasted my time and I always left her office wondering what was that more confused than ever. So I approached Judith instead and I was able to land my dream job within couple of months. Thank you so much Judith!

I was struggling for almost 2 years looking for a job. I was at COSTI first but my employment consultant didn't really do anything for me and was very hard to get a hold of so I decided to switch over to Next Steps and it was a WORLD of a difference. The staff there are so knowledgeable and helpful. Genevieve is my employment consultant and she advised me to attend the workshops and I am glad I did because Judith is AMAZING! She is so good at her job and really understands what we need to be successful in our job search. Within a few months, I landed a job, finally!!!! I am now working in my field and I couldn't have done it without the help of the wonderful staff at Next Steps! If you have been struggling to find a job, go there!! They will give you everything you need to help you with your search but you have to be open and willing to do what they say! Thank you, Next Steps!!

Had an absolutely incredible experience with Next Steps Employment Centre. I went to the Ontario Works Office in a crisis, unsure of what to do next in my current situation and had no options for employment. Ended up meeting Judith and Linette, two absolutely incredible women who were able to inspire me and give me hope for the future. Had a meeting with Belinda a few days later to discuss employment options and the situation I was dealing with, and ended up getting some amazing resources to get me ready for a new job. Incredibly grateful for the support and encouragement I received here, so many resources are available for people who just need a hand in the right direction. I highly recommend them!

Genevieve Graham is THE WORST coach EVER. All she did was waste my time. She didn't tell me about all my options. Another coach there has to tell me there was a program I could go back to school and start a career. When I approached Genevieve about it she was clueless. Then thanks to Google she found it and said I would probably qualify. Twice she wasted my time with useless research, saying I needed to do this to be accepted. Each time I brought my research back to her she would say 'oh, you also have to do this...' like why not tell me everything at once so I can move forward? Truly infuriating. I guess if you're born here and white they don't want to help you. She even screwed me over on classes. She circled only three on my calendar then when I went to apply to that program to get back to school she says I don't have enough 😂 of course I don't have enough, you didn't register me.
Do not use this center!! They only string you along so it looks like the office is busy and they can get all their nice salaries and long lunch breaks. You will have better success using Google schooling and other online courses. If you are trapped here and Genevieve is assigned to you switch ASAP

When I came to Canada I struggled a lot to get a job opportunity. I didn’t know how to approach things in Canada, then I came to know about next step employment service Center. They conducted many free sessions and taught us a great deal on how to create a better profile, building connections and how to be self- motivated and be positive. It helped me a lot to have confidence and then it let to a job opportunity in Canada. So I am very thankful for their service and my mentor Judith Hart who did the great job by showing me the right path. I recommend everyone who is looking for a job in Canada to reach out to them to get a positive result.

name: Toronto District School Board – Next Steps Employment
address: 1225 Dundas Street East Mississauga ON L4Y 2C5
description:
Horrible experience. Obvious that it caters to new immigrants leaving Canadian citizens helpless. Communication is terrible and you're made to feel like you're the problem for asking for help. The purpose of this place is to help people find work or prepare them to get back into the work force, they will do everything in their power to do NOTHING and blame you for not doing their jobs.

I came here to be a part of the better jobs Ontario program, because of the lack of communication and unwillingness to help, I wasted nearly 3 months of back and forth thinking I was going to get support... I am now without EI or any source of income for the foreseeable future because they refused to help.

When I asked if there’s anything sooner and expressed politely! that I’m a bit disappointed, the lady seemed reluctant to actually help me. I actually canceled work just for this appointment, and I was waiting for it for several weeks. I wish I could sue because now my chances to get into the program for September are almost zero.

name: Tropicana Community Services Organization
address: 505 Consumers Road, Suite 102, North York, Ontario M2J 4V8
description:
My experience with Tropicana Community Services was extremely disappointing and frustrating. I was referred to this agency through Ontario Works and was required to attend an initial appointment without having a choice.

My assigned employment counsellor was Ronilca Glasgow. From the beginning, the process felt like a waste of time. The location is inconvenient, and being required to attend in person was unnecessary. During the first appointment, I was asked personal and mostly irrelevant questions for almost an hour. I was told to email my résumé, which I did immediately, but I did not hear back for over a month.

Despite the lack of communication and support, Ronilca Glasgow repeatedly contacted me asking whether I had found a job, even though no meaningful employment assistance was being provided. I did not receive relevant job leads, interview preparation, or guidance that matched my qualifications. When job postings were sent, they were often unrelated to my experience or field.

Communication was inconsistent and unreliable. I was often told things like “I will send this to you” or “I will follow up,” but nothing was ever sent and these promises were forgotten. However, whenever I reached out or questioned the lack of support, responses suddenly became frequent and overwhelming.

At one point, I began receiving an excessive number of emails in a single day, many containing irrelevant job postings that did not match my qualifications. It felt less like support and more like harassment. The focus seemed to be on monitoring whether I was employed rather than actually helping me gain employment.

Overall, Tropicana Community Services failed to provide meaningful employment support. Constantly asking if I had a job while offering no real assistance made the experience stressful and unproductive. Ontario Works should reconsider forcing clients to attend this agency. I am considering filing a formal complaint.

I do not recommend Tropicana Community Services to anyone seeking genuine employment help.

My Employment Counsellor’s name is Ronilca Glasgow. Ontario Works connected me with this agency, so I had no choice but to attend the first appointment. First of all, the location is bad, and having to visit in person itself is annoying. It was a disappointing experience. It was a waste of time, and they asked me boring personal questions for an hour. They told me to send my resume by email, so I sent it right away, but I didn’t hear anything for a month. During that time, I found a job on my own and contacted my counsellor to tell them I didn’t need any help because I got employed. The unbelievable thing was that the person who had no contact for a month suddenly responded immediately, and the tone was threatening. They said I have a one-year contract with them, so I must send my employment contract from the company I worked for and keep updating them about my new job for a year. Of course, I refused and told them again that I found a job and didn’t need any help. From then on, the real harassment from this agency started. They never shared any job opportunities, but as soon as I contacted them, they started bombarding me with 20 emails a day. They even sent me emails about jobs in areas unrelated to me, deliberately clogging my inbox. Ontario Works must stop forcing clients to go to this terrible agency. I plan to file a formal complaint.

I had a very disappointing experience with Tropicana Employment Agency. I came to them because I urgently needed work—but they did the opposite of what I needed.

My first appointment was spent answering background and statistical questions—like when I came to Canada, where I’m originally from, marital status, etc. It lasted nearly an hour and barely touched on job opportunities.

At my second appointment, I met with an employment counsellor who asked questions like, “Do you think you’re better off working or unemployed?”—which I found both insulting and unnecessary. I’m not even on social assistance. Why would anyone come to an employment agency if they didn’t want to work? It felt like they were checking if I was freeloading rather than offering help. The counsellor then filled out a government form and asked a few things about my resume—again, more paperwork, little action.

After that, I met with a job developer for about 5 minutes. I told her I was looking for work in security, and she seemed uninterested. She referred me to a company offering floater security guard jobs—on-call, unstable shifts. The interview was on March 22, and orientation isn’t until April 10. That’s not helpful for someone who needs income now. When I said I needed something quicker, I was told, “All jobs take at least 3 weeks to hire,” which I know is not true. I’ve been hired much faster before.

Later, the job developer sent out a group email with job postings. I replied showing interest in one—no response.

On top of all that, parking isn’t free at their location. I had to pay $4.25 just to attend appointments while unemployed. That’s a financial burden that shouldn’t exist at a place meant to help people who are struggling.

In the end, I found a job on my own, with no help from them. Tropicana wasted my time, gave me the bare minimum effort, and treated me like just another number. It honestly seems like they care more about collecting government funding than actually helping people find jobs.

The Ontario Ministry of Labour should look into this agency’s practices. If you’re looking for real help, go somewhere else.

Pray that you wont get Ronilca she does not reply does not pickup her phone or if she replies you get some gibberish way to go Tropicana.

Absolutely useless and unprofessional agency (at least the person I was assigned to, not gonna name her). Promised they have IT job for me, never heard from that job, they never bothered to get back to me about it, I had to ask them.

Then I was interested in Better Jobs Ontario program and I had to go through them (as I was registered here). She collected my information and never bothered to do anything with it. She finally jumped up and said she sent documents for the program, when I approached her saying I want to close registration with Tropicana.
Then she mentioned registration closure takes one week (seriously?). I asked her if she could let me know when registration closure is finalized. She never bothered even though she promised, I had to ask her again a week later.
Completely disrespectful behaviour towards people who are struggling and trying to look for work so they can pay their bills.
I give two stars because the behaviour wasn't out right rude, just not caring at all. I advice to look somewhere else.

name: VPI Employment Services
address: 3443 Finch Ave East, Suite 102, Scarborough, Ontario M1W 2S1
description:
Absolutely horrible experience with these people. From the moment I walked into the offices and from the moment I met Waheed I was very apprehensive and wary. Their approach is catered to newcomers I suppose, or people who don't know any better. What ensued over the next few weeks was appointments and calls which were initiated and carried out, without my approval or consent, and even when I did not reply, new appointments were made regardless. My meeting and correspondences with the employment advisor was filled with dreams and well wishes and fluff and zero action, and zero strategy or any actual advice. Needless to say, I wanted to cancel my request for services. But this office will not let me, they are holding on to my file, because they receive funding from the government. This is a scam, and a front for services. I have never dealt with such a strange operation in all my over 30 years living in Canada and dealing with multiple Employment Agents of this type. To me there is something wrong with operations at this office, from not getting calls answered, to having to wait on simple requests, to the fact that there is no body in the office, job hunting, and the office is not running any hiring events. Even the jobs advertised on their website are outdated and inapplicable. This is really sad, that these centers are operating in this manner, with this lack of professional staff lacking any integrity. I feel like they are putting on a show, and it is infuriating getting any proper answers. All the emails are, let me know if I could help.. but you know how you can help me.. why do you keep putting the onus on me? I have provided you with information, it is up to you to provide me with resources, and help me get a job. Not to tell me my resume needs work, when it doesnt. And treat me like this is the first time I am applying for a job. If you want to attract newcomers, go ahead, but you need to have a diversity of expertise, not just pretend to do so.

Yong Wang Lin and Raison Kuriakose assisted well with my resume through breaking down complex situation to more simplistic forms. I was initially late and missed some of the earlier appointments as they were then flexible in their rescheduling and provided flexible and professional feedback. Thank you for your services.

Best people that will give you the jobs u desire and help with resume appreciate them alot keep being positive to the community stay blessed .

They covered the cost of the basic training, but they completely failed to mention an additional **$80 fee** for the security test and background check. Either they don’t know about it (which would be incompetent) or they omit it on purpose.

When I asked them to help cover that extra fee, they agreed—but after two weeks of delays and excuses nothing came through. I had to pay out of pocket just to move forward.

The employees personally try to help, but the company’s handling of payments is slow, unclear, and misleading. relying on them for funding, and they have hidden costs and delays...

description:

We arrived 5 minutes before our scheduled appointment and waited in the office for more than 10 minutes, but no one came to meet us. Very disappointing service and a lack of professionalism, especially considering their responsibility to follow government rules.

Did we help you find a job?

Yes, VPI was successful in finding me a contract position where I learned a lot about what the duties of what is means to be a Administrative Assistant.

Did we help you return to school/training?

At the time school wasn’t in the plans for me, but VPI Working Solutions reminded me that training and certification is something worth things about.

What was your favourite workshop?

My favourite workshop was all of the workshops because I noticed how well they all integrated together.

What was the most important thing you learned?
The most important thing I learned was communication. Communication in regards to how you frame your resumes to even how you communicate when in a interview.

Would you recommend us to others?

Yes, I would recommend VPI Working Solutions to other people.

Would you come back again if needed?

I would come back to share my success stories.

In the whole year I’ve only received 2 emails with job postings. And, during the one time we did chat on the phone, we discussed follow up phone calls and an action plan. But there was never another phone call and no action plan.

I was surprised to receive an email at the one-year anniversary congratulating me for being with them for a whole year and that their services for me has ended. When I reached out to my Case Worker to question this she said she would still continue to help but never heard from her again!
