It's a farce that this place says you are required to use them to continue to receive social assistance, while making you visit in person for every appointment, while feeding your private information into public AI in order to do their job for them.

I would be better off doing all of my resume creation and job searching on my own, because at least I have knowledge of how to build a resume properly and not just rely on AI to do the work for me and keep my private information safe. I would give zero stars if I could for this idiocy.

Ramien and the entire team at Employment Ontario have been incredibly supportive throughout my employment journey. They consistently checked in on my welfare and working conditions and made sure I felt supported every step of the way. Their professionalism, care and genuine concern truly set them apart. I am very grateful for their help

I give Ramein a 5 stars! she helped me from WCG. she helped my work and school. She is an amazing employee! and she works hard, very detailed oriented and does her best to give her best to her work! 5 STARS for Ramein my caseworker and also to WCG. for helping individuals who need help for school and work. Godbless! may WCG continue help more people seeking job and school.

I think they follow the rules do nothing looks busy. I go there for better job program and more than 3 months there is no result at all. Keep giving me call appointment after 3 to 4 weeks and keep rejecting the courses. They cant even satifsy the client and keep you waiting until you stopped going there. Absolutely no idea how thing works. Cant even answer the one question. Its been more than 3 months and they cant even find how better job program works what is course eligibility and keep stating different things in different calls. Never go they cant help you.

name: WoodGreen
address: 815 Danforth Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M4J 1L2
description:
OMG walking into the first floor of their employment agency felt more like entering a mental health hospital, talking to mental people than a place meant to help people find jobs. The staff seemed unprepared and unprofessional, leaving me more confused than supported. It is incredibly frustrating that, despite receiving government salary funding, they are falling short of their core mission. Not only did they fail to offer the services I was expecting, but I left with no clear direction or support and I had to run away from them. I couldn’t figure out how to give zero stars on Google reviews because, honestly, they don’t even deserve one but to leave a review, I had to click on 1 star.

I have been to the place for Canada child benefit application and I have been informed about every detail very patiently by YAN. She was very kind and knowledgeable. She also had me booked as I wasn't available in person, for an on call meeting to update me with more benefits I can be eligible for.
For a place where there's a lot of misleading and misinformation, this place have been a proper guide to me. I'm extremely satisfied with YAN's customer service and empathy.
Thank you so much for your service.

I called just to ask about tax return and the lady that answers the phone is extremely rude Didn’t even let me finish, saying what I had to say before cutting me off I understand that she might not be  aware. Of what Im asking or what I meant but that doesn’t give you an excuse to be rude to people who are calling. It is not my first time that she has done this extremely rude. I don’t know why if you don’t like your job then get a new one. Or simply say nicely and kindly that you do not know how you could help me,  it’s all about customer service. Other than that great experience

Very disappointed with the lack of professionalism. I had a confirmed appointment and traveled 1.5 hours to their office, only to be told it was rescheduled — without any prior email, call, or message. That’s a total of 3 hours wasted, with absolutely no respect shown for other people’s time or commitments.

Would give zero out of 5 stars if possible. No hot water for 18 weeks last year, regularly no heating, no cold water (below 92 degrees) for the past year, and now in a heatwave, no AC for 3 days+ for the entire building. Everyone at Woodgreen wants to be a manager and "in charge" but no one wants to do any of the work that is required to be a landlord and provide minimum legal requirements for tenants.

name: Working Skills Centre
address: 55 Eglinton East, Toronto, Ontario M4P 1G8
description:
This is a wonderful institution with very experienced professionals, and extremely kind teachers. I recommend you to study here. You will never regret it and they will take root in your heart, so much that you will love them forever. They are really like a loving family, very caring. Even if you don't like math, you will love math with their teaching (Formulation in Excel).

I cannot recommed these courses and teachers enough! they made these sessions very engaging and rewarding. the topics were broken down into manageable parts. the teachers were passionate and willing to go an extra mile to ensure understanding. they were supportive and encouraging. Thank you.

Working Skills Centre is one employment agency committed to their objectives, ensuring participants acquire the right skills required to secure a job. The environment is warm and welcoming, the staff are helpful, in effect leaving you with memorable moments, and increase confidence in your skill level.

This SAP, QuickBooks, booking and excel   training was so good and experience to know about Canadian accounting and finance knowledge  it helps  me confidential for finding a job and get some interview I recommended any one that want to continue in finance work this training was so interesting and helps full .

This is the best place for gaining great skills for employment, like becoming a proffesional receptionist. Silva is an amazing facilitator,  very knowledgeable  and skilled. Thank you WCS

name: YMCA
address: 150 Isabella Street, Unit 201, Ottawa, Ontario K1S 5H3
description:
I was received by mitri and amal, great warm services, thank you so much for your precious help 🙏🏽

Thanks alot Honey for your time &
valuable information

I’m a new comer to Canada and was seeking help for the guidance for a better future. I once had a visit at YMCA information centre and luckily I met Liz Manser and Arvind. Both of them are the best people I have ever met and the way they helped me is unforgettable. They helped me in finding a right path and also helped me for applying job. For job they helped me from starting to the end. Whole staff is awesome but my favourites are Liz and Arvind. Anyone needs help, please visit YMCA.... they are very best and helpful.

Mitri and Brenda have been helping me very much and I appreciate their service to me.

Wonderful center. @Amal has been a great supportive representative.

name: YMCA of Greater Toronto
address: 4580 Dufferin Street, Suite 200, North York, Ontario M3H 5Y2
description:
Extremely disappointing and unprofessional service.

The so-called “job search support” was nothing more than basic CV editing and sending online applications — something that can easily be done independently. There were no real job opportunities, no employer connections, and no added value.

What was even worse, I waited 1.5 weeks for a scheduled English assessment at the Bloor Street location, only to be told on arrival that I was not eligible for the test. This should have been verified before my appointment was confirmed.

A complete waste of time and very poor organization.

I met with Kalsoom today. She is very kind and helped me a lot. Thank you so much for your support.

Useless. They only have low-skilled jobs available and act like you're supposed to accept the scaps. They don't actually want to help you to get a comparable, decent earning job for which you're qualified for.

Came here today with my husband for him to do his EAsl test which was great. But we came to the employment side after to get some help. We came and was pushed to fill out an Ontario Employment participation registration. Only to find out there is no one there to help you. No counsellor there to help. The appointments are so far away almost a week later. My understanding is that an employment Center should have people there to help you even for walk ins. IVS and learning enrichment do and they are funded aswell. To come to an employment center and not have anyone to help I don’t think that is normal.  I called to do one of there programs while I was there and the lady told me they were all full rushing me off the phone. And then I asked her for October  classes she said Oo okay but I’m busy now send me a text. Very unprofessional the whole employment part.

Don't expect Employment services, it has been one month no one call back me as the black girl claim. Even I had been there, not busy at all.

name: YMCA of Greater Toronto
address: 10610 Bayview Avenue, Unit 12, Richmond Hill, Ontario L4C 3N8
description:
They were very helpful and resourceful. Many thanks to Minoo and a host of others working at this location.

I’m amazed with how kind, useful, effective, comforting and successful YMCA Staff is, specially Case Manager Jordan, He has done such a great job and also inspiring! Highly recommended! Every single person there shine on, helpful and hands to work 100%

To all people who are desperately looking for a job and think they can get help from YMCA, if you think this way, well just don't! You won't get any help from them, they're getting paid to help you with your resume, skill development and job search but all they do is blaming you and accusing you and monitoring you instead of helping you, don't waste your time and energy and stay away from them.

The manager and all the workers at this center do nothing. If you go into the center, it’s always empty because they are inactive and don’t even know the YMCA policies.

Very kind and welcoming place, they will help you find any job!

name: York Construction Academy
address: 155 Champagne Drive, Unit 8a, North York, Ontario M3J 2C6
description:
York Construction Academy is an excellent place for learning and growth. It is perfect for people who are looking to gain construction skills in Canada, start a construction career, or improve their knowledge if they already have experience but lack the theoretical aspects.

This academy is ideal for those looking for solutions, knowledge, opportunities, a new career, or a better understanding of how houses are structured in Canada. One of the best things is that the courses are offered in short periods of time and with multiple schedules, which makes it easy to study according to your availability.

However, what you gain here is much more than just classes. You gain real experience, strong knowledge, and excellent support. Bora provides great guidance, and the instructors Leonardo, Milan, and Steff are outstanding. They truly care about students and provide support beyond expectations.

Both the theory and practical classes are very detailed and valuable for future development in the construction industry. If you ever need to come back to strengthen a specific skill, you have the opportunity to do so.

Thank you, York Construction Academy. This has been a great experience in my life in Canada. In the end, you also build strong networking connections with classmates, which is amazing because I found much more than I expected — not only education, but also friendships and cooperation.

I had a great experience at York Construction Academy.
The in-class instructors Milan and Steve were excellent at explaining and demonstrating hands-on skills, including framing, basic electrical, drywall, trim installation, flooring, basic plumbing, window and door installation, painting, and demolition. They did a great job breaking down real construction methods and rough framing, along with all the key elements of the building envelope in a way that was practical and easy to understand.
The theory instructor Nardo was outstanding. He taught the theory behind all of the hands-on work, as well as foundations and blueprint reading. His years of industry experience really showed, and he explained concepts in a way that was easy to retain and directly applicable to real job-site situations.
Overall, the instructors were knowledgeable, professional, and genuinely invested in student success. I would highly recommend this program to anyone looking to build or strengthen a career in construction.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Excellent Construction School With Great Training & Guidance

I had a great experience at this construction school. The instructors give very clear, motivating, and practical lessons that make the concepts easy to understand. Their talks are inspiring, and they take their time to explain everything in a way that even beginners can follow.

The environment is friendly, professional, and focused on helping students build real skills for the construction industry. I learned a lot, gained confidence, and feel more prepared for real-world work.

I highly recommend this school to anyone interested in construction. They truly care about their students and provide quality training that makes a difference.

York Construction Academy provides practical, hands-on training with knowledgeable instructors who are passionate about student success. The learning environment is supportive, well-structured, and focused on real-world construction skills. I highly recommend the academy to anyone looking to build a strong foundation and career in the construction industry.

I recently graduated from York Construction Academy and I can say I made the right decision to choose this college. The staff and the teachers are so nice and friendly. Thanks a lot to Mr Milan , Mr Nardo and Mr Steve. They’re not only wonderful teachers but also good humans with a heart of making sure you understand each and everything. It was such a great experience to learn here :)

name: Youth Employment Services (YES)
address: 555 Richmond Street West, Suite 711, Toronto Ontario M5V 3B1
description:
I had a really great experience with YES. The team is incredibly and truly wants to see you succeed. My advice to anyone joining is to be patient and make sure you follow up regularly.

If they recommend any training to help you prepare for work, take them seriously. Those programs are very helpful and can build your confidence and skills.

I especially want to thank Nirosa, Carmen, and Catherine — they are absolutely amazing. They were kind, encouraging, and always ready to help when I had questions or needed guidance. I really appreciate all the support I received.

I had an amazing experience with YES. From the very first session, the staff are welcoming, knowledgeable, and genuinely invested in helping you grow professionally.
They helped with refining resumes, build confidence for interviews, and even introduced you to job opportunities that you wouldn’t have found on your own.

What I appreciated most is how personalized the support feels— they don’t just give generic advice, they take the time to understand people’s goals and guide them step by step. Whether you’re just starting out, switching careers, or looking to level up, YES is an incredible resource.

Highly recommend for any young person looking to take the next step in their career!

I just want to get signed up, I keep getting redirected to a voicemail after talking to the front desk and no one ever calls me back. I’ve tried emailing too. I first reached out over a month ago…

We've had a good experience working with YES, especially in collaborating with Ragul, who's been a great resource. We've hired a few team members through the program who have been hardworking and great support staff.

I personally visited this organization in person, and I did not receive any assistance there. The workplace address provided to me by the staff member was unclear and unreliable, and it felt as though they were simply trying to get rid of me. Additionally, they do not respond to emails. I would not recommend this place to young people.

name: Youth Employment Services (YES)
address: 1610 Bloor Street West, Toronto, Ontario M6P 1A7
description:
I went to this office for information on Better Jobs Ontario. The orientation went smooth enough. However, speaking to Zarah was a very exhausting experience. She continued stalling and giving conflicting information on whether or not the ministry would pay for my courses. I had to end the call before I lost it.

The next day I went to complain to the manager Diane/Diana & she stated she wouldn`t talk to or take action against Zarah since she was 'doing her job'. I don`t know about you but I heard of people who were just 'doing their job'.

THIS PLACE IS A WASTE. AVOID THIS OFFICE.

***UPDATE 26 FEBRUARY 2026***

I was told by Florencia Boronat that I`d be contacted by the Vice President on the week of 26 Feb. I haven`t heard anything from them all week until I had to email them. I feel that since I had to remind them of a situation they take 'seriously', they said the VP would contact me later that day. I figured they put a 'rush' on trying to get this situation dealt with.

Later the VP (Carrie-Anne Goodfellow) did call me as promised. I was (as expected) given the brush off by her. She said (I`m paraphrasing) 'If you didn`t like our service, please go to another office'. There was no real remedy to resolve the issue at hand which was difficult representatives, conflicting information & managers who refuse to take action. Every time I tried to air my grievances, Carrie-Ann kept trying to interrupt or talk over me. This is behavior unbecoming of a Vice President of any business or charity. When someone needs to vent or air their griefs, the best thing to do is listen more than you talk. I don`t thing Carrie-Ann got that memo. She even went on to say 'We are under funded'. This has nothing to do with the issue at hand. If anything, this is a weak excuse to try to dismiss the situation. She even tried to get off the call while I was complaining. I hope Carrie-Ann`s boss reads this review.

The only good thing (if any) about this is that I complained about 3 other offices and this is the ONLY one that had the professional courtesy to contact me. Still, they did the bare minimum & it`s going to hurt business wise in the long run.

THIS OFFICE IS ONE SAD JOKE.

Awful experience as a job seeker here. I don't know how this place remains in operation. They have to find a way to justify government funding, when in reality they are of little use. I never received proper info on 'start-up cost' assistance which used to be handled by OW, despite repeated requests. This is a reflection of widespread dysfunction across Toronto social services. My worker, Desmond, repeatedly delayed answering emails, (once as long as three weeks!) and always had an excuse. Lack of follow-through for job interviews and no response from management when I e-mailed a complaint. Leaving them was a nightmare too: I had to get an OW supervisor to close my account with them! Stay far away from this place, unless you want to waste your time and be continually frustrated!

with the way phone calls are handled at your office. Unfortunately, the receptionist often does not answer phone calls at all, and when we leave voice messages, no one ever calls back.
Additionally, on the occasions when the phone is answered, the attitude is not polite or professional, which makes the experience even more frustrating.
This lack of responsiveness and professionalism makes communication extremely difficult. I hope this issue will be addressed as soon as possible.

I discovered this place about a month ago and filled out the online form on their website to get services.

However, I didn’t get any response or any acknowledgement that they got my responses from them so I had to go in person to fill out their “application” form.

When I went, the receptionist was nice and acted professional.

Somebody should investigate these folks. Not only they do nothing but they promise stuff like clothes when you get hired and they never deliver on their promises.   Don't waste your time here and if this operates with tax payers money an investigation is due.

name: Youth Services Bureau
address: 1355 Bank Stret, Unit 703, Ottawa, Ontario K1H 8K7
description:
Almost all the bad reviews are from parents who are upset their child isn’t doing EXACTLY as they say. One parents is upset their child if dressing the way they want rather than “the way they’re told to dress” that’s absurd. YSB is a great service and they’ve helped me lots. I’ve been through YSB for several rounds of counselling. Not only that but the walk in too and I’ve had nothing but good experiences. Sometimes it takes some time to find a counsellor you click with but just be patient. YSB is a great support for those suffering from mental health problems. I’ve also had support from Cheo and I would 100% recommend YSB over Cheo unless it’s an emergency situation.

Any time there was a resource there to help me, they fired everyone who made that resource available. The people in charge of this as a business are truly awful people and do not have any people skills or understanding of the real world impacts on individuals who rely on the resource. When I was homeless and needed help with housing, they used to have someone designated to help with phone calls and digging up ads, helping us navigate that. They don't any more.
They also used to have a much wider sexual health care team, and I had so many trans friends who were fired just because they spoke up about issues that mattered. I had long term employees guess my weight openly when registering me as someone who used the service. (I have personal struggles with eating disorders.)

Around this time I also had friends who were volunteers who were doing all the work of full time employees but being paid $20 a week at most. There was significant ableism, transmisogyny, general hostility towards so many of us who never had any options.

Another thing is that they have a policy of not hiring anyone on for pay who has used the resource within the past 3 years. There is absolutely no well-intentioned reason for this rule to be in place. It is inherently a way to make sure that the people who work there never understand what it is like to be a youth relying on the service. And that these youth relying on the service do not get the pathways they need to get out of hard situations. That's not okay.

I have seen far too many people cry because of this business and far too many people overtaxing themselves within an inch of their own lives just because they care about who this organization is supposed to help. Or that they rely on the money involved. But they don't get to say anything about it because the shareholders and business owners hold such a totalitarian grip on it all.

Nothing about us without us is for us.

Very disappointed in this place, my daughter went for counselling to help work through some things. Unfortunately they decided to tell a 15 yr old that she can move out when shes 16 and they'll pay half her rent. My child suffers from anxiety and depression and there answer was for her to move out on her own. Which then caused a ripple effect and she has started losing herself with not wanting to listen, being confrontational not showering or dressing as she was told to dress and do what makes her comfortable. This has caused an issue for school. How far weve come is gone from just 2 counselling apts. I would never refer anyone here if this is there answer to helping with issues. We wanted individual and family counselling but nope sorry I'll find somewhere else to go.

I am shocked at how YSB encourages a teenager ( raised by me since 2 years old and very well adjusted until now ) to leave their primary parent when they 'take their cell phone away' and encourage them to be with the good-time parent with more $ for toys. parent alienation is a real thing and so is the abuse i have been through. The  fact that YSB enables  teenagers to not see the the true light of reality is beyond me. So, if others are considering YSB to help their family, I would suggest another path. It will cost $ but worth it in the end. Please don't count on YSB for anything other than allowing your teenager to take total control and undermine you as a parent. I would like to add that there was a severe, written, breach of my parental privacy here which will have ever lasting effects.

If I could give ZERO, I would.
To think this is where CHEO is sending people?!
I was told that I was the problem, not my child because I told her phone/wifi privileges away when she ran away.
There has to be somewhere else for parents to turn to.

name: YWCA Toronto
address: 2425 Eglinton Avenue East, Suite 303, Scarborough, Ontario M1K 5G8
description:
For me YWCA is blessing. When I came canada, I was so depressed and stressed because of not having a job here. But when I met Anu who is the employment consultant in YWCA, She helped me a lot. She helped me to get a job. She helped me improving my resume and also gave me suggestions how can I face interview. I am thankful to Anu and also YWCA for helping me getting a job. In canada I started my first job with their help so I will never forget them in my whole life. They are so friendly and supportive, for me specially Anu. I highly recommended this YWCA Toronto Employment service at 2424 Eglinton ave E, Scarborough.

Do not come here and seek employment. The worker assigned to me, Tasleen, is a very nasty person. I did not like her vibes. I could tell from the start that I was not welcome, and I came here for a second time in 2024-2023, seeking employment or some kind of help getting back into school. Tasleen belittled me in front of everyone and told me I was unfit to work. Meanwhile, she does not email you, makes you wait for months, and lies about promising jobs that get your hopes up for nothing. She is always on vacation or hiding in her office, avoiding you. I believe the other staff are also involved because, despite reading from other people's complaints, it is pretty disgusting. I pray whoever is in charge of this YWCA opens their eyes and finds new workers to replace these types of workers. This should not be happening in Canada, as we are a multicultural society that should respect others regardless of religion, race, or learning ability. Everyone is entitled to work and look for work, not be degraded or discriminated against by these job agencies. Tasleen also had the nerve to tell me verbally that she only works with people who have a bachelor's degree because they are smarter than a high school graduate or someone with a disability. Well, Tasleen, I hope everyone sees this message, and I hope the higher authorities see it and have you removed from your position. From my experience as a client, I could tell you do not like Christians, white people, or anyone with a high school degree or disabilities. If I were your boss, you would have been fired immediately. This type of behavior should not be allowed or accepted in any job agency. You are there to help others, respect others, and find their happiness in work. Please do not come here. It's toxic, discouraging, and discriminatory.

I have been waiting for employment support from this center since May.
Unfortunately, the response has been very slow, and despite calling and emailing multiple times, I received no reply.
My agent, Anu, promised to call me once but failed to follow through several times.
This situation has been very difficult for me and has delayed my job search process.
I hope the center’s management will improve their communication and organization so others can receive help more easily.

Aside from Tamara (who does workshops and one on one resume help appointments, she is amazing), the assistance I received from this center was mediocre.

The staff here (other than Tamara) just seem to be coasting by, trying to do the least. They give off the impression that they have no genuine desire to help, no care for clients beyond numbers to serve a quota, no interest in your goals, and no enthusiasm for their line of work.

In several instances, I was left immensely disappointed and frustrated. People who seek assistance are already struggling. The last thing we need is to be dismissed and discouraged while we are trying to get back on our feet.

Toronto is a huge city, and there are many organizations out there that demonstrate genuine care for their clients. I got connected to a training program that's helped me a lot through an entirely different organization, which I didn't have to pay anything for.

My advice for anyone who is considering working with this center is to be your own biggest advocate. Make use of the government funding that you are entitled to. They may try to sell you on their paid courses or recruit you into a training program unrelated to your goals in order to fill their own quotas.

It's a shame that I had such a subpar experience here because I have a lot of respect for the YWCA's mission of empowering young women, so I hoped for better.

This review is almost a year after the period I received the service. It has been great, I got help in reviewing my resume, and guidance on writing cover letter etc, which helped me in my job search. I also received assistance for CPR certification, which increased the value of my CV. I’m thankful to YWCA and particularly to my advisor, AS, who was very welcoming and supportive.

name: John Howard Society of London & District: Main Office
address: 601 Queens Avenue
description:
Very straightforward and professional. They solely exist to help people who don't have anyone to in their lives to guide them through certain legal processes. Signing in for probation because of a court order, they will sometimes give you the option of calling in. That definitely helps if you're like me or anyone who works full-time.

Thanks again guys your awesome and appreciated.

I need to express my sincere appreciation for all the kindness and support from Brynn, Vogue, and Tracey. I will forever remember how John Howard Society was always helpful and thoughtful and professional.
September 2009 - July 2025.
All the best Tracey 👍🏻

Hannah helped me for over a year. Couldn't be more thankful for JHS.

Excellent staff, very helpful, respectful and knowledgeable. Great guidance for all aspects of life or any issues you might be dealing with. I highly recommend for everyone.

Great organization! This location is small, with a tight parking lot in the back. They suggest parking down off Adelaide.

name: First Work
address: 895 Don Mills Road, 2 Morneau Shepell Centre, Suite 900, Toronto, ON M3C 1W3
description:
This company is a total scam. They exist as a third party adjudicator for short term and long term disability. It doesn’t matter what documentation you’ve provided them, they will simply deny your  claim without any rhyme or reason unless you are terminally ill and on your death bed. When you question any decision made, they will ignore your emails and calls at all costs. Interesting how they allegedly have a doctor review the information who you also have 0 ability to connect with. I would avoid any employer who uses this company for claims at any and all costs. You better have a good lawyer ready to fight these folks.

The absolute worst financial management company I have ever had the misfortune of being involved with. For reasons unknown my employer decided to switch our RRSP plans to this company. Upon a first time HBP RRSP withdrawal request, I learned the true incompetency of this firm.

I filled out all documentation on 04/30 - the agent assured me withdrawal requests would process in 2-3 business days as I had mortgage conditions due on May 7th. On Monday May 3rd my employer verified my withdrawal request, which would have allowed ample time for this company to submit my withdrawal request in time.

As of today May 7th (mortgage conditions due) I have been told the documents were not filled out correctly (when in fact they were, it was simply an encrypted T1036 from gov't Canada), and I have been denied 4 separate management escalation requests by 4 different agents over the past 72 hours.

As of this writing, I have not spoken to a single administrator, supervisor or manager, and have been given the run around by every single employee I've spoken with. Their customer service is appalling, utterly repulsive. The rage I feel towards this company cannot be put into words. They are extremely lucky I had access to funds in lieu of my RRSP withdrawal to meet our closing conditions on the sale of this property.

This company is not even BBB accredited, they should not even be legally allowed to manage anyone's money, let alone receive CORPORATE contracts for hundreds, to thousands, of employees. I sincerely hope this company goes bankrupt.

STAY AS FAR AWAY AS POSSIBLE.

The employees in the mail room were helpful in, pointing in the right direction.
The office itself wasn't,  closed it's doors long time ago and didn't leave a forwarding address.
Bad individual experience dealing with the insurance company,  constant delays and misinformation

These case managers really don’t care about you. I’ve dealt with 3 so far and all of them are the same. You can never get a hold or reach them unless they want an updated medical form from you. They don’t care about your recovery or help you in anyway. All they do is question why you are “totally disabled” and unable to work the job modification they make for you. All they do is acuse your illness of being “acute” and request medical forms every other week ( knowing it costs $50-80 ) to support your absence and advise you your conditions are outside of “their guidelines to recovery “ even though my doctor advises otherwise.

These people arent here to help, they are a “third party company” that assists your employer to pressure you to go back to work even if you arent ready or able to.

The Managers are making medical decisions on restrictions of employees, even though medical documents are submitted they ignore medical advice.
You will have to get legal consultants and pay to get claims approved. Or restrictions removed. I would never ever recommend this useless company to anyone they take your money but don't pay you when you need them.

name: Alpha Career College
address: 25 Tangiers Road, Unit 1, North York, Ontario M3J 2B1
description:
Alpha Career College has been an important part of my journey toward success and a meaningful step in helping me achieve my goals. The education provided by the college, along with the hands-on practical experience, has been extremely valuable and well-aligned with real-world requirements.
I would like to express my sincere gratitude to all the instructors, especially my mentor, Marsha, whose guidance, support, and positive attitude made the learning experience truly enjoyable and motivating. I also appreciate the entire Alpha Career College team for being cooperative, professional, and friendly throughout the program.
A special thank you to my November 2024 cohort—the friendships, classroom discussions, and shared experiences made this journey memorable and joyful. I truly miss our time together.
Alpha Career College is truly a gateway to success. I highly appreciate the institution and confidently recommend it to others who wish to achieve their career goals within a short period of time. The college provides excellent placement support and effective strategies to prepare students for their future careers.
Thank you to the entire Alpha Career College team for your dedication and support.

I had a wonderful experience with my placement coordinator, Allison. She was organized, approachable, kind, and very supportive throughout my PSW placement. She answered all my questions, and always responded to my messages.

She guided me through every step of my PSW placement with calmness and clarity, which made the entire process feel smooth and stress-free.

name: Steve Mapletoft Career Consultant
address: Sudbury, ON
description:
Absolutely LOVE working with Karen and her team at Life Zone! I worked with Karen myself when I found that I needed some coaching and worked to clear those emotions and limiting beliefs that were  holding me back. I have also had opportunity to refer clients to Karen. The results have been WONDERFUL! Karen works with her clients by meeting them where they are and helping them get to where they want to be, utilizing best practices techniques in addition to coaching skills she continues to hone. With her constant focus on her own personal and professional development, Karen is able to provide a holistic approach to her clients, including nutritional counselling and amino acid therapy. I highly recommend Karen for therapy and coaching - whether challenged with severe trauma or just looking for some coaching to move forward, Karen can help!

Her coaching practice focused on supporting executives during these challenging times.  I have heard from a variety of people about how she has helped through various difficult situations.  Highly recommend!!

Simply put, Changed my life.
Post traumatic growth w/Neurologist Programming.

Karen helped me navigate through a difficult time in my life. Karen helped me look internally to see things about my life that I needed to understand and change in order to begin a new chapter in my life.  She assisted me in setting new goals as well as dealing with past things that were stumbling blocks in my life she also helped me use those stumbling blocks to become stepping stones to overcome those obstacles that had been buried and not dealt with. She help me realize there were things I could change and something I needed to let go of, there were behaviours from my parents and grandparents that were not good.  They had become like chains holding me back from experiencing freedom to move on.
She also helped me deal and face issues about how I regarded myself.  I was not as forgiving to myself as I was with others. I needed to give myself some slack.
I also learned, am still learning I need to treat myself like I treat my friends and strangers with kindness and respect.
Karen has been a knowledgeable and solid help. No one plans to go through separation and divorce later in life. Also some people don’t realize how things/ events, habits from parents grandparents play such a bearing on our growth and development. Karen walked me through these events and help me realize them and deal with them constructively with these situations.
I would consider myself a man’s man. Not a big show of emotions or expression.
I have learned it’s good to show emotions, it’s good to show expression and most of all I have a voice and an opinion and it matters!
Karen has always been quick to respond to my calls for assistance  and follow up.

Thank you Karen
Gio

Working with Karen at Life Zone has been life-changing, and I truly cannot put enough emphasis on that. My life right now is infinitely better than it was before.

Before working with Karen, I did not fully understand how much anxiety I had been carrying since childhood. She helped me uncover those patterns and change the internal language I was using to process them. The anxiety I had been carrying is no longer just reduced, it is gone. I still deal with normal day-to-day frustrations, but painful moments from my past no longer have a hold on me.

One of the clearest signs of that change was how naturally I was able to remove alcohol from my life. Karen has also given me practical tools that have helped me in my relationships and in my work, and those tools have truly borne fruit.

I am incredibly grateful for Karen's care, insight, and support. This experience has been transformative, and I would highly recommend her to anyone seeking real and lasting change.

I was very pleased with service. They are very helpful. Amazing stuff members. They helped me with everything they could like resume, cover letter and in general they went along with me to help me with my journey as I was new to the city. Especially to Kaitlyn. Thank you

name: Lawrence Square Employment & Social Services
address: 700 Lawrence Ave. W., East Tower Entrance, Suite 330, 3rd Floor, Toronto, ON M6A 3B3
description:
I’m rating 5 stars for my previous caseworker Christina McKeon! From the very beginning of my file here Chrissy has showed me nothing but love and compassion. She is the definition of someone who loves their job and going to work because she knows she is helping others and making a change. Chrissy takes her job seriously and really uses every resource she can to help those in need. This girl deserves a raise. I got transferred to a different worker 2 months ago and I called Chrissy for help today and she is in fact still going out of her way for me to get the help I need and feel comfort.

I recommend Christina McKeon to any member of Ontario Works she’s stayed late to help me, she went above and beyond to help ensure my funds were correct for my housing, she has showed me the boutique the office has where I was able to pick out a jacket and some clothes for me and my son… truly a blessed person and I pray all good karma comes her way.

If you ever see this,
Thank you for being you Chrissy, don’t change for nun! Nuff love.
-Bree
:)

The desk clerk (not the one of Asian descent) that was working Nov 26th at 12:40 when I visited is disgustingly disrespectful and hostile. If you hate your job, find another one. You’re making everyone’s life harder than it needs to be for no reason. She hangs up the phone while you’re in the middle of speaking so I simply showed up to the office to speak to her respectfully in person, seeing as she has the nerve to treat people the way she does over the phone. Majority of this staff needs a course & re-training in both customer service AND communication. This is disgusting behavior given the nature of the job.

If you are assigned to this location based on your postal code, ADVOCATE for yourself & stand up to these people humbly and respectfully because they will absolutely test your patience and disrespect you at this location.

Case worker 415 miss A. George is a professional who takes pride in her job she goes above and beyond with helping her clients grade A service. We all need a ms George in our lives !

The workers are so full of attitude and themselves. They hang up on u, they don’t even know anything about the work they’re doing. They need a dam leash (training) terrible staff!!! They should be dealing with animals.

Giving two stars instead of one because my intake worker was very kind and compassionate towards my situation, very patient and I've experienced good attitude from front desk. The issue I have is with the current caseworker C.F. My file gets put on hold because I applied to EI but my employer hadn't cooperated and given me my Record of Employment, which I tried to resolve with Service Canada as I was told to do. C.F. tells me he can't take my file off hold because I need a letter from Service Canada that my EI application is progressing- which they have repeatedly told me they don't provide but they will in my case. He continuously leaves my call to voicemail on days I know he is available at the office, and calls me back days later- if at all. He has shown a lack of empathy to my situation and I get the feeling he has biases towards my ethnicity, or my age. He tells me that he is unable to put my file off hold because of their EI worker, but this is false. I do not think he follows standard procedure in this aspect. I called the supervisor of this office to file a complaint, and that sent me straight to voicemail. Within the hour of trying to vocalize my complaint against my caseworker, I receive a call from him trying to rectify the issue finally. It's very suspicious to me that he knew I tried to file a complaint against him, which leads me to believe it's not confidential. There are definitely some caseworkers who are not like this. But my overall experience with this office has been a hassle from the very start.

description:

It was my best decision ever to complete my bachelor’s here.

Already missing this place.

No stars . They have no staff on campus , even if you call you wont speak to anyone ( no mater what day or time ). Because they are not there They let the don have physical relations with students in the dorms. They let random people off the street in the dorms. They have no security at the doors. They put my daughter in thousands worth of debt and no help what so ever from student services or the finance department (Charity Meiklejohn) .they locked her from her grades and accounts. They put her to collections.  Not only are they trying to crush her dreams but now they are trying to ruin her credit. They are evil and trying to ruin kids futures. They except these kids to do and know everything after not having to be independent before university. Or students that have social anxiety and or not yet comfortable talking to bully , aged adults yet . This was her first yr and they've almost ruined her life's dreams and goals. We've had no help from anyone at Trent with this and im on disability. Disgusting,absolutely disgusting to do to a young girl just trying to make it and be successful in life . Shame on Trent

One of the top university here in canada, provides different fields of study program. They have good facilitator but the cost of the college is high compared to others

As a second-year Bachelor of Business Administration student, I’ve had a really positive experience at Trent University Durham GTA. The Business professors are fantastic—they genuinely care about our success and do a great job preparing us for the professional world. They’ve been approachable and supportive, and I’ve even had one of them act as a reference for a recent internship application, which I really appreciated.

Deborah, the School of Business coordinator, deserves a special mention. She’s super friendly and incredibly helpful, whether it’s sorting out course selections or pointing you toward internship opportunities. It’s nice to know there’s someone like her looking out for us.

The small class sizes have made a big difference for me. It’s easier to participate, get to know people, and build connections—which is important, especially in business.

As a commuter, I have to say the free parking is a huge bonus. It’s one less thing to worry about, and it makes getting to campus so much easier.

Overall, it’s been a great place to study and grow, and I’d definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a more personal university experience.

Trent University in Oshawa has been a disappointing experience for us. They initially promised a co-op program, which was one of the main reasons my wife chose to pursue her Master’s degree there. However, after arriving at the university, they informed us that the co-op option was not available. Instead, they offered a small research opportunity with a promised payment of $500 but after taxes, she received only $380.
This is a message for future students: please do thorough research and speak directly with past students before making any decisions about studying at Trent University. Don’t rely solely on what is advertised get real feedback from those who have actually studied there.

description:
A lil late in writing this review, but here I go. This review in particular is for a person named Tracy counter #15. Life had been hard for me for awhile and to encounter her, had made my year! (no joke) She showed such gentleness and kindness which I'll never forget. Without going into too much detail, I needed help with my health card and drivers license. It was a bit complicated and she made the whole process easy and seamless. I'm so very thankful that she was so kind, that I had cried quite a bit behind closed doors. I'm forever grateful to you Tracy! Management please read this, she does her job extremely well and deserves recognition of some sort. I, myself have worked in retail management for over 30 years. So I know excellent customer service when I receive it! Give her an award, raise, praise...something! Hopefully next time I visit, I'll be lucky enough to be sent to you Tracy. I wish you all the best!

It's decently sized and has 22 service points, so you don't need to worry about spending an eternity in queue. We went there for an OHIP card and finished everything in less than 30 minutes with about 40 people in queue.

Very fast service even for walking and no appointment, u can still get in and leave in max half an hour , they have like 40-50 counters so they call numbers.  Very quickly but if u want u can always make an appointment online just to be safe but overall friendly service and very quick

Lethargic staff.
Extremely long waiting times.
Irregular ticket call to windows, even for the same service.
No advance appointment booking available at any day/time for over a month.

Just had my 3rd coversation with Service Ontario regarding the issuance of the Green Book for Construction with amendment inserts for complete legislation up to 2022.

1st representative said this publication was available with inserts for $16.00

2nd representative said we can't sell you the amendment inserts with the January 2020 book without having been notified by Occupational Health & Safety that they are available (he said there was not notification within depts that these inserts were ready) so I asked him to put me through to Occupational Health & Safety.

Final representative at Occupational Health & Safety didn't understand what I was asking so we took a bit more time straightening that out which I was happy to do for our mutual benefit.   When she understood what I required, she said the January  2020 edition with amendment inserts was available now fat the cost of $8.00...as I tried to reconcile this half price, she unfortunately displayed an unprofessional manner like I was getting on her last nerve.    I told not to be irritated because I just had to have the correct information (for my report to the prospective purchaser of several copies).   I still don't get why all three people had completely different information.

I don't believe any of them care if we make the purchase with them or not...they still get their pay cheque and their superiors wouldn't have a clue that all three gave me different answers to the same question.   Does anyone have to answer for this apparent pandemonium at Service  Ontario or do the taxpayers (who pay their salary) just have to accept this quality of service?

A female receptionist, who does not were a name tag, does not provide her name, accessed my personal information without my consent and treated me in a disrespectful, racist, and Ukrainophobic manner. One times, she printed my personal information from the computer during my meeting with a caseworker and started to shout at me that i printed without giving me a minute to talk. She does not have any customer service skill like listen respectfully to clients, but she talks and screams loudly and the customer try to respond to her talk. I was standing at the receptionist and she talked to the staff without any acknowledging of me presence and any apology for waiting or notice to be help shortly. Short, this Russian vulgar receptionist decided that she can shout at in in the public in the presence of other clients whereas she is paid from the  taxpayers public money to provide service to the public including me. I was extremely outrage and right away told to cancel my file with the Access Employment!

A.J. Lockhard, Program Coordinator, was vulgar, verbally abusive, and refused to provide the receptionist’s name when I asked. Also, she is vulgar and racist who does not teach her staff proper customer service!

My privacy was violated when my information was printed and read without my knowledge or approval.

I was subjected to harassment, intimidation, and threats, and discriminated against because I am a Ukrainian refugee with disabilities.

This conduct is unacceptable, unsafe, and discriminatory. I have demanded that my file with ACCES Employment be closed immediately.

I strongly caution others, especially newcomers and vulnerable clients, before trusting this location with your personal information or seeking assistance here.

Wouldn't let me use the printers because he claimed I wasn't registered even though I have been registered with access for a long time. On top of that he was very rude. Management needs to speak to this employee. That is not how you deal with clients in a professional manner.

For new immigrants to Canada, ACCES offers one of the best services with respect to preparing you for the Canadian job market.
There's loads of workshops to help with resume writing, interview prep, Canadian workplace cultures, etc.
The staff are very nice and really helpful.

EVIS EVIS 😇amazing! She has helped me twice in reaching out to them and guided me step by step to communicate with the right people. I truly appreciate her support and hope that everyone can be as helpful as she is. I also hope to secure a job through their assistance. Thank you!
Update after one month 😁
I had a very positive experience with ACCES Employment. Maha Bandeali was professional, supportive, and kind throughout our communication. I truly appreciate the guidance and encouragement I received. Thank you for your great work and dedication!

ACCES is a terrible agency that doesn't care about helping Canadians. Their program to give employment at Toronto Pearson Airport is highly discriminatory against Canadian citizens, as their priority is people who recently arrived in Canada. They are perpetuating the poor service quality at the airport because newcomers don't know how things are done in Canada. This is a real shame because Pearson is the largest and busiest airport. ACCES Employment receives an insane amount of money from the government, private companies and other donors, but does not work for Canada or Canadians.

A special thank you to Lubica for her professionalism and encouragement. I honestly couldn’t believe how much I learned in just one week ; from job search strategies to interview preparation and networking.
This program not only builds skills but also confidence. I’m very grateful for the support.

I want to give a special thank you to Lubica Halamova, who was incredibly supportive, knowledgeable, and always willing to help. Her guidance made a big difference in my learning experience.

Highly recommend this program to any newcomer looking to build confidence and gain practical job search skills.

You'll end up on onboarded (why?)  and get a few emails. Ultimately the employers who use this service are seeking a training stipend for hiring target candidates.

Interpret that as you will and don't waste your time

They've been getting this feedback for a long time.

This place is a Waste of Tax Payer Money. People here Do not help Canadian Job seeker but only foreigners & refugees.

No options for Canadian Laid off workers with education, not qualified for their IT training, Better Jobs. Everything ONLY for work permit people, foreigners etc

This place should be Closed !!!

The team at here is knowledgeable and supportive. They helped me identify my strengths and develop a job search strategy. This boosted my confidence level. Special thanks for Ms Orin Chowdhury. Highly recommend their services specially for job seekers!

name: Korean Canadian Women’s Association
address: 27 Madison Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M5R 2S2
description:
I've visited KCWA family and social services today and also last week for my PR application, staff that was assigned for me: Ms. Kim was very helpful, organized, and it was free of charged consultation. Staff also said they do the translation service and lots of other services, if you are Korean and want to consult regarding life in Canada, you should consult with KCWA staff :-)
Thank you for your help.

I was provided with a great service from this institute. I was lost, not knowing what to do, but the KCWA staff helped me out. Ever since then, I did some volunteer work there and saw them work. I was impressed by their mind. They wanted to help people as much as possible and really cared for their clients. I learned a lot from them and it was a greatful experience.

It may look like a small place, but it is not! Social workers here are very passionate, professional and caring - they will willingly sit with you to work on your problems even for a long time. If you live in the vicinity, I recommend to check their monthly seminars as well - they offer a variety of professional seminars from how to file taxes to how to apply for a citizenship. Thanks KCWA for your service to the community, and please keep up the good work!

I think that KCWA is one of the most useful orgarnizations for immigrants in Canada. I would like to share my experience.

1. I got some very useful information posted by KCWA when I was a new immigrant.
There were many, but the most wonderful information was about a free government program. I took a horticulture technician course for 8 months at Humber college, and I could have the first job experience in Canada after the completion of the course.

2. KCWA helped my family and me to renew PR card and apply for the Canadian  citizenship.

3. I attended some useful KCWA seminars such as legal guide, tax guide, and education workshop etc. Besides, I was able to participate as a volunteer for the education workshop.

4. My children took some classes such as Essay class, and French class at KCWA. They were very helpful, too.

Thanks to KCWA~♡

Genuinely good people who work here. I always got friendly services when I needed help.  I would like to thank Gloria Choe for her help. She helped me to get my permanent resident and I finally got my PR last week. Thank you!

This place is amazing and so good for special needs kids. The staff are great.  My son is autistic goes here for his dentist visits.

Got an opportunity to visit the hospital and volunteer for a day here. Well maintained facility for kids to support them throughout their life

Thanks to my PT at Casey House for the referral to the orthotics department at Holland Bloorview.  Easy COVID screening protocols, flew through admin and onto my appointment.  Resident was extremely professional, thorough and kind.  She very diligently walked me through the entire process (pun intended) right through to booking the follow-up.  I feel like I'm in good hands here.  Beautiful facility.

We had the absolute pleasure of working with Charise MacDonald and Kristyn Lee as our seating advisors for our child, Ali—and we couldn’t be more grateful. From the very beginning, Charise and Kristyn showed a deep understanding of Ali’s unique needs, approaching everything with compassion, professionalism, and a genuine desire to help.

They took the time to listen, ask the right questions, and make thoughtful recommendations that truly prioritized Ali’s comfort, safety, and independence. Their expertise was evident at every step, and they worked seamlessly as a team, making what could have been an overwhelming process feel smooth and reassuring.

Ali lit up during the fittings and interactions—it was clear that Charise and Kristyn weren’t just doing a job, they were building trust and making a difference. Their attention to detail and ability to balance clinical knowledge with heartfelt care truly set them apart.

We feel so fortunate to have had them on this journey with us, and we wholeheartedly recommend them to any family looking for support in seating and mobility. Thank you, Charise and Kristyn, for treating Ali with such dignity and for making such a positive impact on our lives.

name: Canadian Filmmakers Distribution Centre
address: 144 Front Street W, Toronto, Ontario M5J 2L7
description:
Great place with cool movies (nice people too)

Canada's best independent film distributor .

CFMDC and their screening space, Cine-Cycle (absorbed by CFMDC after a long tenure as an independent screening space) offer great, responsive, affordable service for independent filmmakers and media artists.

C-C is a tucked-away gem in the city, showing artists' work you're unlikely to see elsewhere in Toronto, including at its proliferating film fests, and in any of the old-school, celluloid formats too. Recommended.

name: Augmentative Communication Service, Ontario Crippled Children's Centre
address: 350 Rumsey Road, Toronto, Ontario M4G 1E8
description:
We had the greatest therapist, tech support was amazing. But when we had issues where the school violated our child’s human rights they 100% took the schools side and did not support our child. The school needed so much more AAC training and Holland Bloorview released our son to wolves without reservation. We can never forgive them for that.

They have a process you can request with your child’s records called a lockbox. I would definitely look into that.

name: Ontario Human Rights Commission
address: Dundas Street West, 7th floor, Toronto, Ontario M7A 2R9
description:
Very rude phone operator to my son who is special needs. I couldn't believe they didn't help just don't want to do their jobs. He ended up hanging the phone up from the rude lady.

I mean, all one needs to understand exactly what this organization actually is to listen to the 7 minute long process before they put you on hold for 1+ hour ( at best) and count the number of times they tell you are either 1. in the wrong place 2. what they will not help you with 3. how you must behave 4. are you sure you're in the right place should I continue. Well done OHRC well done.

OHRC has refused to investigate my complaints regarding discrimination and unlawful emailed threats against me by Furnal Equinox, a convention in Toronto. There really is no point in filing these sorts of complaints with them because they'll just throw you under the bus without offering real solutions.

I was refused entry into a bookstore despite me declaring my medical exemption status to not wear a mask. I complained to a Human rights rep, who had the gull to tell me that since the store was offering me curbside pick up, they were not refusing service..they were refusing to give me an accommodation. and that the curbside pick up was the accommodation.
and that if a store refuses to serve you outside then you call us back.

called back months later after a popeyes chicken refused to serve me outside. OHRC rep told me that I failed to provide proof that I have an exemption so they did not have to serve me period.

Unlike Public Health and By-Law which claim proof is not required. OHRC believes that you have to show proof of a medical condition, otherwise anybody could just say they are exempt, but will allow biological men to go into women's spaces like locker rooms.. if they just declare they identify as females. OHRC is so woke they can give a bottle of Ambien a sleepless night.

It's a scam!!! First thing out of the chair person ( aka judge) was " Full disclosure, I did not read any of the documents". After only my testimony she had her mind made up.. no name calling no case. You would think someone in that position would know discrimination is not limited to name calling. I paid over $30,000.00 of lawyer fee and this "judge" couldn't be bothered to read the documents that OHRC had me on tight deadlines to produce. Minorities are at the mercy of the majority, if they allow us to have rights that fine but thinking we have enforceable rights is a illusion.
